phase: R0 — arbitrary-amount kill switch with change notes
circuit: policy_tx_2_2 (2-in/2-out, value conservation sumIns + publicAmount === sumOuts)

single_input_plus_change.scenario: deposit $50, pay $25 to merchant, mint $25 change note, burn input

two_input_plus_change.scenario: deposit $10 + $50, combine both, pay $45, mint $15 change

value_safety.scenario: forge: pay $25 from $50 but mint $40 change (sumOuts 40 != 25)

clean_funds_preserved.result: change note shares payer identity; spending it passed the ASP membership gate; clean by construction

note: Change support is purely witness + pool-insertion. The existing circuit already enforced value conservation, so a change note is just output[0] = (inputs - payout, payer pubkey, fresh blinding) with publicAmount = -payout. The pool now inserts non-zero output commitments as leaves and emits ChangeNote events; the spend-side fixed-denomination check was removed (deposits stay fixed-denom). The +17M CPU vs baseline is the extra Poseidon2 tree insertion for the change leaf.
